subject: science
title: Student with hearing impairment in my Class 6 Science class
query_text: Student with hearing impairment in my Class 6 Science class

immediate_steps.title: Immediate Action
steps: Seat the student in the front row, slightly to the side where they can see your face and lips clearly during explanation
Use the blackboard more than usual — write key science terms, steps, and diagrams visually for every concept you teach orally
Make eye contact with this student when speaking and avoid turning your back to the class while explaining

classroom_activity.title: Classroom Activity — Visual Science Vocabulary Cards
classroom_activity.activity: For any new science topic (e.g., parts of a cell, food chain), write each key term on the blackboard with a simple hand-drawn diagram beside it. Ask ALL students to copy these into a personal 'Science Word Book' — a few pages folded together. The hearing-impaired student uses this as their reference. Pair them with a buddy who can tap their shoulder gently to signal transitions or instructions.
classroom_activity.materials: Blackboard, chalk, notebooks, pencil — nothing extra needed

inclusion_strategy.title: Inclusion Strategy
inclusion_strategy.strategy: Use a 'show-don't-tell' approach: demonstrate every experiment or process physically before explaining verbally. Assign a consistent peer buddy who sits beside the student and uses simple gestures or written notes to relay instructions. This benefits all visual learners in the class too.

reflection_prompt.title: Reflection
reflection_prompt.question: After today's class, did the student appear to follow the lesson at the same pace as others — and what one visual support made the biggest difference?

source_citation.title: Source
source_citation.reference: NEP 2020, Section 6.11 — Inclusion of children with disabilities; assistive and language-appropriate teaching-learning materials; peer support strategies aligned with NCERT inclusive education guidelines
